characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Death Seeker: It's implied that Malcolm is this because of the trauma of being a serial killer's son. Experienced Protagonist: A veteranFBIagent and serial killer profiler who's forced out due tobeing reckless and charging in without backup. Hidden Depths: Revealed in "Scheherazade" that his knowledge of ballet is from the time he took classes as a kid. I Am Not My Father: Malcolm's driving character trait is his desire to separate himself from his father's influence. It Runs in the Family: Everyone is worried that Malcolm has inherited his father's insanity, including Malcolm himself. As it turns out in the season finale,Malcolm isn’t his father’s son at all when it comes to killing. Ainsley however...

characters.name: Malcolm Bright
characters.description: Death Seeker: It's implied that Malcolm is this because of the trauma of being a serial killer's son. Experienced Protagonist: A veteranFBIagent and serial killer profiler who's forced out due tobeing reckless and charging in without backup. Hidden Depths: Revealed in "Scheherazade" that his knowledge of ballet is from the time he took classes as a kid. I Am Not My Father: Malcolm's driving character trait is his desire to separate himself from his father's influence. It Runs in the Family: Everyone is worried that Malcolm has inherited his father's insanity, including Malcolm himself. As it turns out in the season finale,Malcolm isn’t his father’s son at all when it comes to killing. Ainsley however...

characters.name: Ainsley Whitly
characters.description: Friend in the Press: Always ambitious Ainsly often attempts to use her connections to Malcolm to get inside information on the cases he works in exchange for assisting. Whilst Malcolm is often weary of letting her in and concerned with the lengths that Ainsley is willing to go (including several times putting herself or others in danger), he nevertheless does accept her help from time to time. On occasion, Ainsley even treats investigating the cases as a game to see which sibling can find the solution first. Intrepid Reporter: She is a journalist who is always eager for a good story and willing to do anything to get oneincluding visiting her father. This becomesdeconstructedwhen her cameraman (and boyfriend) is stabbed during a lock-down and Martin performs surgery on him to save his life. He's less than pleased when he learns this as she seems more interested in the story thanthe fact he almost diedand promptly ends things with her romantically and professionally. It Runs in the Family:Ainsley is very much her father’s daughter as Nicholas Endicott discovers to his misfortune. This, however, terrifies her.As of "Face Value", Ainsley tells Malcolm that they'll have to live with the fact that she killed Endicott. As of "Face Value", Ainsley tells Malcolm that they'll have to live with the fact that she killed Endicott. Nice Girl: Ainsley wants to help Malcolm with moving on from their father ever since he was arrested.

characters.name: Jessica Whitly
characters.description: The Alcoholic: She's rarely seen without a drink in her hand, though she is classy about it. The Atoner: She tries to channel her guilt and anger into humanitarian causesand offers a reward for any information regarding the girl in the trunk once she realizes that it wasn't just Malcolm's imagination. It's All My Fault: It turns out that Jessica blames herself for Martin's killing spree becauseshe suspected he was up to something but never acted on it, because she thought he was just having an affair. My Beloved Smother: Jessica is an overbearing socialite who keeps pressuring Malcolm to quit his law enforcement career and get married.

characters.name: Dr. Martin Whitly
characters.description: Affably Evil: He may be a serial killer, but he is remarkably soft spoken and personable to everyone he meets. However, his wide smile and sense of humor also makes his interactions more creepy. Catchphrase: "My boy!"In Like Father...:"My girl!" In Like Father...:"My girl!" Even Evil Has Loved Ones: Martin is a serial killer, but still clearly loves both his children and ex-wife.In Season 2, he gets concerned aboutAinsley having the potential to kill even after Malcolm hides her work.When Malcolm was about to go to private school, Martin gave his consent to do so so that he wouldn't be ostracized by the public. It also includes changing his surname legally. In Season 2, he gets concerned aboutAinsley having the potential to kill even after Malcolm hides her work.When Malcolm was about to go to private school, Martin gave his consent to do so so that he wouldn't be ostracized by the public. It also includes changing his surname legally.

characters.name: Gil Arroyo
characters.description: A Father to His Men: In "Speak of the Devil", he is rightfully pissed at the officers at his precient for not providing JT with backup. He has to shout at them to make his point. Cool Car: He drives a class Ford Mustang. He's heartbroken when the roof is destroyed in "Wait & Hope" since Malcolm had to use it to improvise his safe landing. Famous-Named Foreigner: Famous-named racial minority.According toWord of God, his surname "Arroyo" was taken from former Philippine president Gloria Macapagal Arroyo. Only Sane Man: When Gil thinks Malcolm (and to an extent Edrisa) are getting way deep into their work. Reasonable Authority Figure: He allows Malcolm to join the NYPD as a paid consultant despite his rocky family ties to New York.

characters.name: Dani Powell
characters.description: Action Girl: In several instances, Dani gets in the action to arrest suspected serial killers. Get a Hold of Yourself, Man!: When Malcolm's drug trip goes bad in "The Trip" and he starts having a panic attack, Dani ultimately has to punch him and knock him out. Put on a Bus: As of "Bad Manners", Dani was recruited by NYPD's Vice Enforcement Division.

characters.name: J.T. Tarmell
characters.description: Accidental Misnaming: Malcolm keeps calling JT "Jaime" by mistake. Oh, Crap!: In the Season 2 premiere, JT is mistaken to be an armed suspect by uniformed officers Dani called for backup. It's only after she and Malcolm tell them that JT's a plainclothes officer that they stand down.

characters.name: Edrisa Tanaka
characters.description: Geeky Turn-On: She's quite obviously infatuated with Malcolm, impressed by his forensic knowledge. Why Did It Have to Be Snakes?: Edrisa is terrified of snakes. Which is unfortunate in "Annihilator", as the killer surgically implanted several live Black Mambas in one of his victims.

characters.name: Vivian Capshaw
characters.description: Beware the Nice Ones: Vivian is one scaary woman after she helped break Martin out of Claremont. Deadly Doctor: She got turned by Martin prior to his breakout out of Claremont and holding him hostage. The Whitlys assumed that she was turned because ofStockholm Syndrome. Framing the Guilty Party:She stabs herself in "Sun and Fun" to frame Martin.

characters.name: David
characters.description: Nice Guy: He helps Martin out when he needs to make calls to his family. But otherwise, he follows the rules of his superiors and tells him no when Martin's not allowed to.

characters.name: Frair Pete
characters.description: All There in the Manual: His actual name, Peter Davis, is mentioned by Arroyo in "You Can Run...". Boom, Headshot!: He's killed by a Marshal in "You Can Run..." in this manner. Sinister Minister: In Season 2, Martin befriends Friar Pete, a former friar-turned-serial killer also incarcerated in Claremont. Villains Out Shopping: Afterescaping the hospital, Friar Pete helps himself to a few games of bowling.

characters.name: The Copycat
characters.description: Jack the Ripoff: The antagonist of the pilot is a former patient of Martin's who became enamored of him and sought to emulate him by copying his kills.

characters.name: The Annihilator
characters.description: Freudian Excuse: The suspect went after his victim because of the need to be officially recognized as family. Murder-Suicide: The killer in "Annihilator" tries to do this when the police track him down, poisoninghis wife and kidsas well as himself. Thanks to Malcolm and Dani's intervention, he fails.

characters.name: Mrs. Cadichon
characters.description: You Killed My Father: She committed her murders to avenge her daughter.

characters.name: The Spree Killer
characters.description: Bad People Abuse Animals:Isaac is a 12-year-old boy who would routinely kill his pet rabbits, but his mother covered it up for him. This escalated to stabbing his own father to death. He later stabs his mom's boyfriendfor not letting him go trick-or-treating. Enfant Terrible:The murderer in "All Souls and Sadists" is a 12-year-old boy with sadistic tendencies.

characters.name: Tevin Standish
characters.description: Axe-Crazy: Naturally, since he's in Claremont for murdering his entire family. Crime of Self-Defense: He claims this as according to Standish his family was abusive and killing them was the only way to protect himself.Naturally, no-onebelieves him for obvious reasons...

characters.name: The Junkyard Killer
characters.description: Would Hit a Girl: He nearly killed Ainsley and Jessica, but Malcolm intervened in time.
